Class SecurityLogger

java.lang.Object
security.SecurityLogger
public class SecurityLogger extends Object

Title: SecurityLogger Class

Description: A singleton security logger that captures security relevant events for the Foundations application. Every event is written to a flat log file (security_events.log) so it lasts between runs, and also kept in an in memory list so unit tests can look at entries without touching the disk. Events covered include login attempts, access control violations, authentication failures, password changes, account creation/deletion, role changes, and suspicious input that might signal an injection attempt.

Every entry starts with a timestamp and a bracketed tag (e.g. [LOGIN]) so the log can be grep'd by event type if needed.

Testing: all public methods are exercised by testCases.SecurityLoggingTests.

  • Method Details

    • getInstance

      public static SecurityLogger getInstance()

      Method: getInstance()

      Returns:
      the singleton SecurityLogger

    • logLoginAttempt

      public void logLoginAttempt(String username, boolean success)

      Method: logLoginAttempt(String username, boolean success)

      Parameters:
      username - the username submitted during the login attempt
      success - true if the login succeeded, false if it failed

    • logAccessViolation

      public void logAccessViolation(String username, String attemptedAction)

      Method: logAccessViolation(String username, String attemptedAction)

      Parameters:
      username - the user who attempted the unauthorized action
      attemptedAction - a short description of what was attempted

    • logAuthFailure

      public void logAuthFailure(String username, String reason)

      Method: logAuthFailure(String username, String reason)

      Parameters:
      username - the user the failure is associated with
      reason - a short explanation of why authentication failed

    • logPasswordChange

      public void logPasswordChange(String username)

      Method: logPasswordChange(String username)

      Parameters:
      username - the user whose password was changed

    • logAccountCreated

      public void logAccountCreated(String username, String role)

      Method: logAccountCreated(String username, String role)

      Parameters:
      username - the new account's username
      role - the role assigned at creation (e.g. "Admin", "Role1", "Role2")

    • logAccountDeleted

      public void logAccountDeleted(String adminUsername, String deletedUsername)

      Method: logAccountDeleted(String adminUsername, String deletedUsername)

      Parameters:
      adminUsername - the admin who performed the deletion
      deletedUsername - the account that was deleted

    • logRoleChange

      public void logRoleChange(String adminUsername, String targetUsername, String role, boolean granted)

      Method: logRoleChange(String adminUsername, String targetUsername, String role, boolean granted)

      Parameters:
      adminUsername - the admin making the change
      targetUsername - the user whose role is being updated
      role - the role being modified
      granted - true if the role was added, false if it was removed

    • logSuspiciousInput

      public void logSuspiciousInput(String username, String field, String value)

      Method: logSuspiciousInput(String username, String field, String value)

      Parameters:
      username - the user who submitted the input (can be null if not logged in)
      field - the name of the field that received the suspicious value
      value - the suspicious value itself

    • getLogEntries

      public List<String> getLogEntries()

      Method: getLogEntries()

      Returns:
      unmodifiable list of log entry strings in the order they were added

    • clearLog

      public void clearLog()

      Method: clearLog()

      Description: Clears the in-memory log list. Does not touch the log file on disk — only the in-memory copy is affected.
